_protocol.purpose: Test whether a DJZS FAIL verdict predicts a WRONG real-world forecast more than a PASS verdict does.
integrity_rules: thesis must be REAL reasoning from a cited source — never invented, never edited to add or remove flaws
resolution must be the REAL, verified outcome of the question
select entries OUTCOME-BLIND: choose for rich reasoning first, attach the outcome second, then INCLUDE ALL — never drop an inconvenient one
aim for a mix of CORRECT and WRONG outcomes so the matrix has both classes
keep thesis a faithful, concise representation (~50-300 words); cite source_url; do not reproduce long verbatim passages
_protocol.scope_caveat: Forecasting rationales exercise only the epistemic/structural/temporal codes (S/E/T). Execution/incentive codes (X/I) cannot fire without a position. This tests the REASONING half of the taxonomy.
_protocol.decision_rule: lift = P(wrong|FAIL) - P(wrong|PASS). SIGNAL (suggestive) if lift>=15pp & correct direction. n<10 = UNDERPOWERED. ~20 to attempt, ~40 for confidence.

_format_example.question: Will <event> happen by <date>? (placeholder)
_format_example.thesis: PLACEHOLDER — replace with a real, cited forecaster rationale. Do not run this entry.
_format_example.collection_note: Shape reference only. The runner ignores _format_example; only entries[] is scored.
